diarized_transcript:
[Agent] Thank you for calling HoYoverse Player Support for Genshin Impact. How can I help you today?
[Caller] Hi, I wanted to ask how the submit-a-ticket process works for a bug. Do I have to call, or can I do it in-game?
[Agent] You have a few channels. You can submit a ticket directly in-game through the Feedback option in the Paimon Menu, through the official support website, or by phone here. For bug reports we generally recommend the in-game option because it automatically attaches your UID and your current server region, which speeds up investigation.
[Caller] Do I need a screenshot?
[Agent] A screenshot or a short screen recording is strongly encouraged for any bug report, especially for lost items or interrupted events. Our team uses the timestamp and the UID in the image metadata to match the bug against server logs, and that often determines whether compensation can be issued.
[Caller] What if I don't have a screenshot, just remember roughly when it happened?
[Agent] A ticket without a screenshot can still be opened, but the investigation takes longer and the outcome depends on whether there's a confirmed incident matching your timeframe. If it's tied to a Known Incident on the support page, compensation is usually automatic. Otherwise it goes to manual review.
[Caller] Okay, that makes sense. I'll grab a screenshot before I submit. Thanks.
[Agent] You're welcome! Anything else?
[Caller] No, I'm good.
[Agent] Have a great day!
